title: Event menu option value: client sends raw UI row index as 0x5B EndPara, not script-computed option (Signet never applies)
description:
Symptom: talking to a Conquest gate guard/overseer, navigating the menu to 'Receive your nation's Signet', completes the event but no SIGNET status icon appears.

Evidence (server received & processed EVENT_END, but applied nothing):
- Packet log shows 0x052 (GP_SERV_COMMAND_EVENTUCOFF) + 0x0df (HP update) after the menu select — exactly the tail of vendor/server/src/map/packets/c2s/0x05b_eventend.cpp:71-72. So OnEventFinish ran.
- No 0x063 status-icon packet follows -> addStatusEffect(SIGNET) never called.
- LSB grants signet only when option==1: vendor/server/scripts/globals/conquest.lua:1352-1356 (overseerOnEventFinish). Server derives option from 0x5B EndPara (0x05b_eventend.cpp:38).

Root cause: the client sends EndPara = the raw 0-based UI cursor row index.
- ffxi-client/src/view_native/text_input.rs:2439 confirm_dialog_choice(cursor.cursor) -> AgentCommand::EndEventChoice{choice}
- ffxi-client/src/session.rs:1307 / 1318 build_subpacket_event_end(.., choice) puts choice into EndPara (build_subpacket_event_end at session.rs:3039, buf[8..12]).
But the conquest option codes are script-COMPUTED, non-contiguous (signet=1, supply=65541..65565, exp rings=32933..32935) and depend on getArg1 (conquest.lua:651) sent in the 0x034 event packet. A row index can never equal these.

Two faithful gaps:
1. The 0x034 EVENTNUM params (8x i32 nums = startEvent args incl. getArg1) are decoded (session.rs decode_event_0x034, DialogState.nums) but NOT passed into the event VM. EventVm::start (ffxi-event/src/vm.rs) zero-inits work arrays, so the script computes its menu/option from zeros.
2. OP_SENDTAG (0x43) — the opcode that conveys the script-computed option to the server via FUNC_SendPendingTag — is stubbed as a no-op in ffxi-event/src/vm.rs:181-185. The session instead synthesizes a single EVENT_END with EndPara=raw index.

Faithful fix (design): inject 0x032/0x034 event params into the VM's work/reference region (per research/XiEvents getworkofs routing); implement OP_SENDTAG to emit the EVENT_END(0x5B) with the VM-computed EndPara/Mode; stop overriding EndPara with the UI row index. NOTE FUNC_SendPendingTag's EndPara computation is not fully reversed in research/XiEvents — needs empirical trace of a real conquest event (install present; zone 241 Harara overseer event 32759, see ffxi-event runner.rs test). Must not regress simple dialogs (talk/yes-no) which currently work because raw index coincides with the expected option.
notes:
FIXED & verified against authoritative spec + real game DAT.

Root cause refined: the 0x05B EndPara is NOT the menu row index. Per research/XiPackets/world/client/0x005B (reversed retail client): Mode 0 non-cancel -> EndPara = Work_Zone[1]; cancel -> 0x40000000; Mode 1 -> Work_Zone[1]. The event script reads the selection from Work_Zone[0] and COMPUTES the option code into Work_Zone[1]. The client was sending the raw cursor row index instead.

Empirical proof (Harara, zone 241 Windurst Woods, event 32759; install present): first menu (msg 9050) = 'What do you want? / Would you cast Signet on me? / I'd like to set my home point here. / Nothing.' Driving the VM:
  pick 0 (Signet)   -> Work_Zone[0]=0 Work_Zone[1]=1   (== conquest.lua overseerOnEventFinish 'if option == 1')
  pick 1 (homepoint)-> Work_Zone[0]=1 Work_Zone[1]=4
  pick 2 (Nothing)  -> Work_Zone[0]=2 Work_Zone[1]=0
The VM already computes Work_Zone[1] correctly with NO param injection and NO SENDTAG impl needed (those items from the original plan turned out unnecessary for this flow).

Fix:
- ffxi-event/src/vm.rs: add EventVm::work_zone(i) accessor.
- ffxi-event/src/runner.rs: DialogStep::Ended now carries end_para = Work_Zone[1] (0x40000000 on cancel).
- ffxi-client/src/event_dialog.rs: Advance::Ended { end_para } propagates it.
- ffxi-client/src/session.rs: both VM-end branches send build_subpacket_event_end(.., end_para) instead of the raw choice.
- Regression test ffxi-event runner::tests::harara_signet_pick_returns_option_1 pins EndPara==1 (self-skips without install). Passes.

Legacy (non-VM) dialog path still sends the raw choice as a best-effort fallback; conquest uses the VM path. Awaiting user live-server confirmation.
close_reason: Event menu now returns Work_Zone[1] as 0x05B EndPara (Signet pick -> option 1), per XiPackets client 0x005B; verified by real-DAT regression test. Implemented in vm.rs/runner.rs/event_dialog.rs/session.rs.

title: /exit (and window close) intermittently hard-locks on macOS, requiring force-quit
description:
On macOS, issuing /exit sometimes leaves the process wedged (beachball) so the user must Force Quit; shell shows '[1] terminated' only after the kill.

Repro log:
  reqlogout send (0x0E7) kind=LogoutOn
  disconnect-watcher: returning AppPhase to Launcher kind=Forced   (~1 frame / 15ms later)
  [1] terminated   (user force-quit)

Hypothesis (grounded):
/exit -> SlashOutcome::QuitWithLogout(LogoutOn) at slash_commands.rs:599-603. In apply_slash_outcome (text_input.rs:944-956) it fires THREE things in one frame: cmd_tx.send(ReqLogout), cmd_tx.send(Disconnect), exit.write_default() (AppExit).

The Disconnect crosses to the tokio session, which breaks its loop and emits Disconnected{reason:'agent requested disconnect'} (session.rs:1319-1321). That reason does NOT start with 'server logout state=', so classify_disconnect_reason returns Forced (mod.rs:336-341), and return_to_launcher_on_disconnect (runs in_state(InGame), mod.rs:448-473) sets NextState(AppPhase::Launcher) — racing the already-requested AppExit.

When both land in the same teardown window, Bevy runs a full InGame->Launcher transition (OnExit(InGame) despawn_ingame_entities + drain_* at mod.rs:198-206, then OnEnter(Launcher) launcher_backdrop/launcher_ui spawn NEW GPU/Metal resources) concurrently with winit's exit on the macOS main thread. Issuing Metal work against a surface mid-shutdown is a classic intermittent main-thread deadlock -> 'sometimes'.

Ruled-down secondaries: no spawn_blocking in client/dat/audio/nav (tokio runtime drop should be fast); session task & folder_task JoinHandles are discarded at mod.rs:544-545 so cleanup relies entirely on Disconnect delivery; bevy_audio(rodio/cpal/CoreAudio) teardown possible but Bevy-managed; Bevy AsyncComputeTaskPool DAT read on a slept FFXI_DAT_PATH could stall task-pool shutdown.

Immediate step (this bead): add an exit watchdog + teardown checkpoints to positively confirm WHICH path stalls (winit/wgpu vs tokio runtime drop vs lingering thread). Watchdog arms on AppExit, and if the process has not finished teardown within N seconds, logs the last checkpoint and SIGABRTs to capture an all-thread crash report (~/Library/Logs/DiagnosticReports). Likely fix (follow-up): /exit and /quit should request AppExit WITHOUT routing through the Disconnect->Launcher path (or gate the disconnect-watcher when an exit is pending).
acceptance_criteria: Watchdog converts the hard-lock into a logged abort with a captured backtrace identifying the stuck teardown stage; root cause confirmed; minimal fix removes the AppExit-vs-Launcher race.
notes:
ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED via watchdog + crash report (~/Library/Logs/DiagnosticReports/ffxi-client-2026-06-22-215120.ips): main thread wedged in winit internal_exit -> World::clear_resources -> <RenderAppChannels as Drop>::drop -> event_listener wait -> pthread_cond_wait. It is a Bevy pipelined-rendering shutdown deadlock (render-thread handoff never completes), NOT the tokio runtime drop and NOT (causally) the disconnect->Launcher transition (that watcher fires on EVERY /exit since /exit always sends Disconnect, so it was correlation, not cause). last_stage stayed 'AppExit observed' (app.run() never returned).

FIX APPLIED (user chose): disable bevy::render::pipelined_rendering::PipelinedRenderingPlugin in DefaultPlugins (ffxi-client/src/view_native/mod.rs). Removes the RenderAppChannels resource entirely so clear_resources has nothing to block on; preserves graceful shutdown. Tradeoff: render no longer overlaps simulation (small always-on frame-time cost; relevant to kuluu-p09). Builds clean; fmt+clippy clean. Watchdog (exit_watchdog.rs) kept as permanent backstop (should now never fire).

STATUS: fix landed, NOT yet runtime-verified. NEXT: run release build, /exit ~10x to confirm no hang and no watchdog abort.

title: Self character model doesn't update on equipment change (ensure_self_lookcomp clobbers server look)
description:
User: character model should update when equipment changes; does it support other players?

Findings: the look->model pipeline (LookComp Changed -> dispatch_look_driven_models -> respawn skinned mesh) already routes EVERY entity's look. Other PCs update on gear change because the server broadcasts their CHAR_PC (0x0D) look and sync_entity_looks_system applies it to LookComp. The SELF player did NOT update because ensure_self_lookcomp_system runs after sync_entity_looks_system and every frame forces the self LookComp back to the launcher-time SelfAppearance (never updated post-launcher), clobbering the server-driven look. session.rs:670-736 already decodes+upserts the self look (incl. on equip). 

Fix: ensure_self_lookcomp_system now SEEDS the self look only when absent (before the server CHAR_PC for self arrives); once present, the server-driven sync owns it. Strictly better: identical when server look is None (falls back to launcher look), correct when present (gear changes re-render). NEEDS visual verification in GUI (can't run headless).

title: Equipment screen polish: icon alpha, cell layout, races off-by-one, MON->MNK job code
description:
Follow-up fixes after item DAT lookups landed (kuluu-2he), from user testing:
1. Item icons rendered opaque black background: icons are 8bpp flag-0x91 bitmaps whose palette carries FFXI half-range alpha (index 0 = A0 transparent bg, art = A128). parse_graphic forced 0xFF for 0x91. Added map_image::parse_graphic_icon (PaletteAlpha::HalfScaled) used by item_dat::decode_icon; general bitmaps unchanged.
2. Grid icon offset + label overlapping icon: cell laid icon+label side-by-side (row flex) and label was always shown. CellLabel now hidden when the slot has an item, so the icon centers alone.
3. Races off-by-one: item DAT races field is 1-indexed by race id (Mithra=bit7 0x0080, Galka=bit8 0x0100, all=0x01FE). Table was 0-indexed so Mithran Gaiters read 'Galka'. Fixed + verified vs retail DATs.
4. 'MON6' -> 'MNK6': status_panel::job_abbrev truncated the full name ('Monk'->'MON'). Scrape canonical 3-letter codes from LSB job_name.lua first element into ffxi_proto::job_names::abbrev (JOB_ABBREVS table); status_panel + item_detail::format_jobs now use it (unified, dropped hardcoded table).
Tests: job abbrev canonical, races 1-indexed, icon alpha verified vs real DATs. fmt+clippy clean.
close_reason: Fixed and verified vs retail DATs: icon alpha (transparent bg), grid cell layout (label hides when item present, icon centers), races 1-indexed (Mithran Gaiters -> Mithra), canonical job codes (MNK not MON) scraped from LSB.

title: Launcher graphics settings menu: compact rows + responsive scroll so it fits any window size
description: The graphics settings menu (ffxi-client/src/view_native/launcher_ui/graphics.rs) renders ~22 fixed-height rows in a non-scrolling panel; on smaller windows the list overflows and is clipped at top and bottom (screenshot). Rows are tall because labels wrap to 2 lines at the 160px label width with the theme default font. Fix: (1) explicit compact row font + no-wrap label sizing + tighter gaps/widths; (2) cap the panel at a fraction of viewport height and move the settings list into a scroll_y region (ScrollPosition + mouse-wheel system) so the title/hint/footer stay pinned and the list scrolls at any window size.
notes: Follow-up: added a visible scrollbar. Wrapped the list + a 6px scrollbar track in a horizontal Row (align stretch). Uses bevy_ui_widgets Scrollbar{target=list, Vertical, min_thumb 28} + CoreScrollbarThumb (ScrollbarPlugin already active via feathers UiWidgetsPlugins) — plugin sizes/positions the thumb from the list's ScrollPosition and makes it drag-scrollable. Rounded track (subtle) + lighter thumb; new GraphicsScrollbar marker + update_scrollbar_visibility system hides the track (Display::None) when content fits so a full-height thumb never implies scroll on a non-scrolling list. BorderRadius is a Node field in Bevy 0.18 (not a standalone component). Build + clippy clean.
close_reason: Graphics settings menu made compact + responsive: smaller row font, no-wrap labels, viewport-capped panel with a scrollable settings list (pinned title/footer). Builds + clippy clean.

title: Resolve {Choice:N}[a/b] dialog alternatives instead of leaking the raw marker
description: dmsg decodes control code 0x0C to a {Choice:N} marker and passes the following literal [opt0/opt1] bracket through; clean_display leaves the marker unresolved, so NPC speech renders 'Good luck, {Choice:0}[citizen/comrade].' instead of 'Good luck, citizen.'. Resolve {Choice:N}[a/b/..] to a single alternative in the render layer. title: Item DAT lookups: correct per-type ROM files + O(1) id-base indexing; fix unequip showing Gil
description:
Follow-up to kuluu-051. Item icons/details never rendered because ITEM_DAT_FILE_ID (0x0DA9-0x0DAC) resolved to tiny wrong DATs and the loader indexed by raw item_id*stride. Reworked to the real model (verified vs XIM InventoryItemParser + empirically against retail DATs):
- Item DATs are per-type, id-ranged, gap-free 0xC00-block files: ROM/118/106-109.DAT (item/usable/weapon/armor), ROM/174/48.DAT (currency), ROM/286/73.DAT + ROM/301/115.DAT (expansions). block index = item_id - base_id (base = block 0's id). New ffxi_dat::item_dat::ItemTable (open/lookup/icon), O(1), on-demand 3KB block reads.
- Client item DAT jobs field is 0-INDEXED (bit==job; White Belt=0x04=MNK), unlike LSB item_equipment.jobs (1-indexed). Fixed item_detail::format_jobs to consume DAT-native 0-indexed.
- Gil bug: unequip showed 'Gil' because the server reports an empty equip slot as inventory index 0 (charutils.cpp:2268), which resolve_equipment joined to Gil. EquipUpdated now clears the slot when container_index==0.
Tests: ItemTable verified against real DATs (White Belt/Bronze Dagger/Breastplate/Gil all resolve name+icon+desc); equip_updated_index_zero_clears_slot; format_jobs 0-indexed. fmt+clippy clean.
close_reason: Implemented and verified against real retail DATs + XIM reference. Icons/details/descriptions resolve; unequip no longer shows Gil.

title: Head-look (facetarget) not sent or applied: bidirectional gap vs LSB
description:
FFXI head-turn-to-look is driven by the entity 'facetarget' field (the targid a char has selected), relayed by the server in both directions. We handle neither.

OUTBOUND (retail can't see our head): build_subpacket_pos (ffxi-client/src/session.rs:3354) builds the 0x015 client position packet but never writes facetarget. LSB GP_CLI_COMMAND_POS (vendor/server/src/map/packets/c2s/0x015_pos.h) places uint16 facetarget at body offset +22 (after dir@+20 and the TargetMode/RunMode/GroundMode bitfield@+21). We leave buf[22..24]=0, so the server broadcasts facetarget=0 for us and retail clients never turn our head. Fix: thread the player's current target targid (act_index) into build_subpacket_pos and write it at +22.

INBOUND (we don't turn other players' heads): PosHead decodes flags0 (ffxi-proto/src/decode.rs:113) but never extracts facetarget = (flags0>>17)&0x7FFF. Both 0x0D (char_update.cpp:271 Flags0.facetarget=m_TargID) and 0x0E (entity_update.cpp:321 ref<uint16>(0x1A)=m_TargID<<1) pack it into flags0 bits 17-31. The renderer (ffxi_actor_render.rs:1847) instead aims non-self head-look at bt_target_id (the combat claim UniqueNo, set only during combat, wrong id-space), so other players only head-track in combat. Fix: add PosHead::facetarget(); plumb face_target:u16 onto state Entity -> wire Entity -> snapshot; resolve it by act_index/targid in the renderer and use it as the non-self look target.
notes:
LANDED (verified), pending merge to main. Split across two places due to the shared-tree churn:
1. ffxi-proto/src/decode.rs (PosHead::facetarget() + 2 tests) is ALREADY in main HEAD (1f90540) — another session committed the whole file, sweeping in my hunk alongside their CliStatus work. Confirmed present.
2. The other 13 files are on branch 'facetarget-headlook' commit b0a7fe3 (worktree /Users/jon/src/ffxi-facetarget), based on e7af8b4.

FULL GATE PASSED in the isolated worktree (vendor symlinked, reused target cache): ffxi-proto pos_head tests (incl. facetarget) pass; ffxi-viewer-core + ffxi-client(native-window) build clean; cargo fmt --check clean; 245 ffxi-client lib tests pass; clippy clean on all 4 crates. git merge-tree shows facetarget-headlook merges into current main with no conflicts.

REMAINING: merge facetarget-headlook into main once the main working tree is clean (it currently holds other sessions' uncommitted work in some of the same 13 files, so a working-tree merge would collide; a merge now could also be reverted by a subsequent foreign whole-file commit). Command when clean: git merge facetarget-headlook. Do NOT force-merge into the dirty/churning tree.

title: Vana'diel clock: elemental day-of-week orb sprite (menu frames 106–113)
description:
Retail draws an elemental orb sprite immediately before the clock time, indicating the Vana'diel day-of-week's element (the colored circle in the reference screenshot). We compute the weekday today but only surface it as a one-shot toast on day change — no icon is drawn. This bead renders the authentic DAT sprite (full-sprite approach chosen over a tinted-glyph placeholder).

The orb is UI element 'menu    frames  ' at index 106 + element. Depends on the menu UI-element DAT sprite loader (roadmap:hud:menu-ui-element-dat-sprite-loader) to load that group.

Day-of-week → sprite-index mapping: our VANA_WEEKDAYS is indexed in DAY order (0=Firesday); the 'menu frames' sub-index is in ELEMENT order (Fire=0,Ice=1,Wind=2,Earth=3,Lightning=4,Water=5,Light=6,Dark=7). So the per-weekday sprite offset table, indexed by (total_vana_days % 8), is [0,3,5,2,1,4,6,7] (Firesday→Fire/0, Earthsday→Earth/3, Watersday→Water/5, Windsday→Wind/2, Iceday→Ice/1, Lightningday→Lightning/4, Lightsday→Light/6, Darksday→Dark/7); final sprite index = 106 + offset.

Evidence (ours):
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/vana_clock.rs:48-57 — VANA_WEEKDAYS (day order, 0=Firesday)

title: Vana'diel clock: retail HH:MM time + (col-row) map-grid position
description:
Our HUD clock renders one label as 'V {year:04}-{month:02}-{day:02} {hour:02}:{minute:02}' (e.g. 'V 0886-01-15 13:55'). Retail shows only 'HH:MM   (col-row)' — clock time then the player's map-grid cell, e.g. '13:55   (I-7)'. This bead drops the V/date prefix and adds the map-grid coordinate. (The elemental day-of-week orb that precedes the time is its own bead: roadmap:hud:vanadiel-clock-elemental-day-orb.)

Two sub-changes:
1. Time format — reformat format_vana to emit 'H:MM' (retail uses no leading-zero hour, 2-digit minute). v_hour/v_minute are already computed; this is a format-string + test change.
2. Map grid (col-row) — compute the player's lettered/numbered cell from world position + the current zone's ZoneMapRecord (size, x/y offset). We already parse that record from FFXiMain.dll and use it to calibrate the minimap; reuse it here. Player position is the IsSelf entity Transform.

Evidence (ours):
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/vana_clock.rs:106-122 — format_vana currently emits 'V {year}-{month}-{day} {hour}:{minute}'
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/vana_clock.rs:113-114 — v_hour/v_minute already computed; only the format! string changes
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/vana_clock.rs:128-162 — existing format_vana unit tests to update for the new format
- ffxi-dat/src/main_dll.rs:21-28,61-84 — ZoneMapRecord {size, x_offset, y_offset} parsed from FFXiMain.dll
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/minimap/retail.rs:223-237 — current calibration via MainDll::zone_map(zone_id, 0); zone_map_to_aabb at :19-29, MENUMAP_TEX=512.0 at :17
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/components.rs:12 — IsSelf marker; player world pos read pattern at ffxi-viewer-core/src/minimap/mod.rs:264-267 (Transform.translation.x/.z)

Evidence (retail, re-express — do NOT copy):
- research/xim/src/jsMain/kotlin/xim/poc/ui/Compass.kt:56-62 — clock string is 'H:MM   (col-row)' (no date, no V prefix)
- research/xim/src/jsMain/kotlin/xim/poc/ui/MapDrawer.kt:54-63,139-140 — grid formula (below)
- research/xim/src/jsMain/kotlin/xim/resource/table/ZoneMapTable.kt — ZoneMap {size, offset} layout matching our ZoneMapRecord
design:
Map-grid formula (re-express MapDrawer.kt:54-63,139-140; 16x16 grid, columns A.., rows 1..):
  mapPos.x = 0.5 + worldPos.x / size * 512
  mapPos.y = -0.5 + (-worldPos.z) / size * 512
  mapPos.x -= x_offset ; mapPos.y -= y_offset      // zoneMap.offset * -1
  col = ('A' as u8 + floor(15 * mapPos.x / 512)) as char
  row = 1 + floor(15 * mapPos.y / 512)
Clamp col/row into [A..P]/[1..16]; render '?-?' when no ZoneMapRecord for the current zone/subzone (mirrors XIM's mostRecentMap == null). Use subzone 0 as the minimap does today. LSB/DAT-boundary: cite the XIM file and add lsb-invariant-prober tests pinning the grid math against known zone fixtures.
notes:
Implemented 2026-06-30. Clock label now renders 'H:MM   (col-row)' (e.g. '13:55   (I-7)'), dropping the V/date prefix.

- Map grid: MinimapAabb::world_to_grid (ffxi-viewer-core/src/minimap/mod.rs) computes the 16x16 cell via floor(15*uv); reuses the minimap's calibrated world_to_uv, which reproduces XIM MapDrawer.kt:54-63,139-140 exactly because our Bevy world flips Z vs FFXI-native (transcribing XIM's raw formula would invert the row axis).
- PlayerMapGrid resource + update_player_map_grid system in minimap/retail.rs: image-independent calibration from FFXiMain.dll ZoneMapRecord (subzone 0) on zone change; '(?-?)' when no record/player. ensure_dll dedups the DLL load.

title: menu UI-element DAT sprite loader (UiElementResource parser → icon atlas)
description:
Reusable loader for retail menu/UI-element sprites (the 'menu    frames  ', 'menu    compass ', 'font    usgaiji ' groups, map markers, etc.). We have icon-DAT loaders for ITEM icons (ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/item_dat_root.rs:49 via ffxi_dat::item_dat::icon_at) and STATUS icons (ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/status_ribbon.rs:8,25 via map_image::status_icon_at, file_id 87), but NO loader for the named UI-element resource groups that retail addresses by string + index. This bead adds that.

Scope: parse the UiElementResource group out of the four menu UI DATs and expose a draw-by-(group-name, index) atlas, mirroring retail's UiElementHelper.drawUiElement(lookup, index). First consumer is the Vana'diel clock's elemental day orb (see roadmap:hud:vanadiel-clock-elemental-day-orb); it also unblocks faithful retail compass sprites, weather gaiji, and full-map markers, which today use Unicode-glyph stand-ins.

title: Diagnose + fix periodic ~70ms GPU frame spikes (render-thread bound, shadow cascades suspected)
description: Frame spikes to ~70ms (baseline ~13ms) every 1-2s, worse with camera pitched up. perf split: cpu~2ms, render~67ms — render-thread/GPU bound, NOT the snapshot clone (kuluu-p09) or the camera_collision probe (kuluu-5lc; probe 0us on these). Named peak render passes are the sun's 4 shadow cascades (4096^2, max_distance 600 on High) but their CPU encode is <7ms, so the 67ms is unattributed GPU/render-world. Two leading hypotheses: (1) directional shadow cascade GPU cost ballooning when the frustum sweeps skyward (cascade fit), (2) on-demand wgpu pipeline specialization as new geometry/shadow-casters enter view. Next: run with FFXI_GPU_TIMING=1 (kuluu-fgs) to read real ~gpu spans, then pick the lever — clamp cascade maximum_distance/count, or warm pipelines. sun_moon.rs:145 is the only shadow caster; graphics/settings.rs cascade_config_from_settings holds the knobs.

title: Other players' rest states (/heal, /sit) not animated
description: tick_live_ffxi_actors hardcodes rest_kind=RestKind::None for non-self entities (ffxi-viewer-core/src/ffxi_actor_render.rs:1761-1769), so observed players who /heal or /sit are never posed kneeling/sitting. The resting animation byte is already on the wire: the server broadcasts ANIMATION_HEALING(33)/ANIMATION_SIT(47) in the entity's server_status (body[0x1B]), decoded into Entity.npc_state.animation and preserved sticky across pos-only ticks (state.rs:588). engaged/dead/fishing_phase are all already derived per-entity from e.animation in the same loop; rest was simply never wired. Fix: derive rest_kind for non-self from e.animation (HEALING->Heal, SIT->Sit) mirroring the fishing_phase_by_id pattern. Self keeps using local RestStance. SITCHAIR(63-73) left as follow-up (needs chair-anchored clips). Verified against vendor/server/src/map/entities/baseentity.h.
notes: Fixed in ffxi-viewer-core/src/ffxi_actor_render.rs: added observed_rest_kind(animation) helper (HEALING->Heal, SIT->Sit, cites baseentity.h) + rest_kind_by_id map built from e.animation, and replaced the hardcoded non-self RestKind::None with the per-entity lookup — mirrors fishing_phase_by_id. Self still uses local RestStance. Unit test observed_rest_kind_maps_broadcast_animation_byte pins the LSB constants. cargo check/clippy/fmt clean; pose machine (advance_rest_phase) was already per-actor so no further wiring needed. SITCHAIR(63-73) deferred (needs chair-anchored clip). Implementation complete + unit-tested; live in-game visual confirmation (a remote player /heal) still pending a server session.
close_reason: Implemented + unit-tested: observed players' /heal and /sit now drive Heal/Sit rest poses from the broadcast animation byte. Live visual confirm pending.

title: First-person self nameplate dips/jitters vertically (stutter-amplified near-camera overhead projection)
description: In first-person with camera pitched up, the self nameplate billboard visibly dips vertically, and the dips coincide with frame spikes. Root: nameplate world Y = self.y + actor_height + 0.1 sits ~0.28u directly above the camera eye (self.y + actor_height*0.92), a near-degenerate overhead projection. Camera eye is set unsmoothed and the nameplate system reads self-position at a different schedule point, so a 1-frame skew (tiny at 75fps) becomes a visible swing on a 70ms stutter frame. Fix: remove the camera/nameplate skew (shared finalized self transform, deterministic ordering) and/or don't render the self nameplate overhead (vanilla-faithful: own name isn't shown).

title: Perf HUD: enable wgpu GPU timestamp queries so render spans show real ~gpu time
description: perf_hud.rs top_render_spans only ever surfaces ~cpu (render-graph encode) times because GPU timestamp queries aren't enabled. Frame spikes show cpu~2ms / render~67ms, so the cost is render-thread/GPU and currently invisible. Enable wgpu TIMESTAMP_QUERY (+ INSIDE_ENCODERS/PASSES as needed) on the RenderPlugin WgpuSettings so RenderDiagnosticsPlugin records elapsed_gpu, letting us attribute spikes to shadow cascades vs pipeline-compile vs present-block.

title: Equip filter: fits_job off-by-one hides job-restricted gear (White Belt unequippable by MNK)
description: The Equipment menu's equippable-item filter uses the wrong job-bit convention. ffxi-proto::equip_info::fits_job tests `jobs_mask & (1 << job_id)`, but LSB's item_equipment jobs bitfield is 1-indexed: a job occupies bit (job-1). Confirmed in vendor/server/src/map/utils/charutils.cpp:2313,3377 and lua_baseentity.cpp:5082 — all use `getJobs() & (1 << (PChar->GetMJob() - 1))`. Example: White Belt (item 13184) has jobs=2 (bit 1 = MNK), but a MNK (main_job=2) tests bit 2 and misses, so the Waist Equip submenu shows '(no equippable items for this slot)'. Fix fits_job to use (1 << (job_id - 1)) with a job_id==0 guard; add a regression test for White Belt + MNK.
notes: Fixed: fits_job now uses 1<<(job-1) (LSB charutils.cpp:2313). Also fixed item_detail::format_jobs same off-by-one (bit+1) + added jobs 17-22. Tests: white_belt_fits_mnk_not_war (proto), jobs_all_when_unrestricted updated. White Belt (13184, jobs=2=bit1=MNK) now equippable by MNK.
close_reason: fits_job + format_jobs off-by-one fixed; regression tests added and passing

title: PC face/hair off-by-one: resolve_face is 1-based, retail face slot is 0-based
description: resolve_face() in ffxi-viewer-core/src/look_resolver.rs computes FACE_BASE[race-1] + face - 1 (with a face==0 -> 1 special case), making every face index >= 1 render the model one slot too low. The retail face byte is a 0-based index into the per-race Face sub-table: LSB validates creation faces 0..15 ('Face 8B' = 15, vendor/server/src/login/login_helpers.cpp:240) and xim EquipmentModelTable.getItemModelPath indexes the Face slot directly (offset + itemModelId, no -1). Symptom: PC hair color+style wrong (e.g. a red non-pigtail Mithra renders as the adjacent blonde-pigtail face). Affects live look dispatch, DAT preview, launcher char preview, and model viewer (all route through resolve_face). Fix: 0-based index derived from PC_MODEL_IDS slot-0 breakpoints; drop the duplicated FACE_BASE table; tighten bound to face<32.
close_reason: Fixed resolve_face off-by-one: now 0-based direct index (file=base+face) matching XIM EquipmentModelTable + LSB face range 0..15. Derived base/count from PC_MODEL_IDS slot-0 (dropped duplicated FACE_BASE). Added unit tests pinning the convention; added warn! for resolved-but-unrendered equipment files to diagnose the separate headless-PC observation.

title: Zone water spills onto dry paths: per-height AABB union instead of actual footprint
description: Reported in West Gustaberg swamp near Leaping Lizzy: the water surface floods dry paths. Cause: spawn_mzb_overlay groups ALL water-flagged MZB placements by rounded water_height into one AABB (ffxi-viewer-core/src/dat_mzb.rs water_groups), so disconnected water bodies (and a deep pond's higher surface) merge into a single bounding rectangle that covers the dry terrain/paths between them. Each placement's submesh IS its own water-material geometry; render the actual footprint triangles flattened to the water height (depth test clips to terrain below the surface) instead of a merged bounding box. Applies to both the vanilla plane and the bevy_water path. Refs kuluu-980.
close_reason: Closed

title: Faithful fishing: network + animation (observe + /fish minigame)
description:
Re-implement FFXI fishing faithfully. Two phases per the LSB/XIM references:

PHASE 1 (observe other players): render the fsh0-fsh6 macro-state animations from the wire entity animation byte (server_status). LSB ANIMATIONTYPE 56-62 (+pre-overhaul 38-43,50) -> XIM Actor.kt fsh0..fsh6. animation byte already decoded into wire::Entity.animation and persisted across pos-only updates; renderer only consumed it for ATTACK.

PHASE 2 (/fish + minigame for our player): full client protocol state machine.
- start: action packet 0x1A id 0x0E (ActionKind::Fish exists).
- 0x037 SERVERSTATUS carries server_status(animation)+fishing_timer(hookDelay); not yet decoded.
- after hookDelay: send 0x110 mode2 RequestCheckHook.
- server: 0x115 GP_SERV_COMMAND_FISH (stamina/arrow_delay/regen/move_frequency/arrow_damage/arrow_regen/time/angler_sense/intuition) + animation FISHING_FISH; run minigame client-side.
- minigame end: 0x110 mode3 RequestEndMiniGame (para=300 fail/200 force/0 caught/else stamina; para2=special).
- resolution anim (CAUGHT/BREAK/STOP) -> after finish, 0x110 mode4 RequestRelease. mode5 RequestPotentialTimeout near timeout.

Refs: research/XiPackets/world/{client/0x0110,client/0x0066,server/0x0115,server/0x0037,server/0x000D}; vendor/server/src/map/utils/fishingutils.cpp; research/xim FishingAnimationState.kt, ActorSubStates.kt(FishingState), FishingAttemptInstance.kt, FishHppUi.kt, Actor.kt:361.
notes: Phase 1 (observe others) DONE: ffxi-proto animation fishing consts + fishing_phase(); ffxi-actor fishing_clip()+ActorAnimInputs.fishing_phase; ffxi-viewer-core drives fsh0-fsh6 pose from the wire animation byte for observed players. Phase 2 (self /fish + minigame) DONE (network+animation+state machine): 0x115 FishPacket decode, 0x037 server_status+fishing_timer decode, 0x110 build_subpacket_fishing encode, fishing.rs FishingMachine (10 tests), reactor wiring (auto-play + FishingInput), /fish + /hook + /rl + /rr + /reelstop slash cmds, self pose via wire::SceneSnapshot.self_fishing. protocol-conformance-reviewer: PASS, no wire divergences. Remaining: native HUD + keybinds (see dependent bead). All tests green; clippy clean.

title: Skinned-material flags buffer re-uploaded every frame (per-submesh write_buffer churn)
description: upload_ffxi_material_buffers (skinned_ffxi_material.rs) re-encodes + write_buffers each submesh's per-material flags uniform every frame, though material_flags only changes when a graphics setting is toggled (realistic_character_lighting / faithful_shadow_receive, ffxi_actor_render.rs:1894-95). With ~30-40 actors x ~6 submeshes this is ~180-240 of the ~251 write_buffer + 301 StagingBuffer::new calls/frame seen in the --features trace capture (kuluu-p09 follow-up). Fix: dirty-check the flags buffer (store last-written Vec4 per instance_id, write only on insert or value change). Zero visual/behavioral change; cuts the bulk of per-frame GPU buffer churn. joints (animation) + lighting (day/night) stay per-frame.
notes: Implemented in skinned_ffxi_material.rs: FfxiMaterialBuffers.flags is now HashMap<u64, FlagsBuffer{buffer,last:Vec4}>; upload loop dirty-checks (write only on Vacant insert or when material_flags.flags differs). bind group reads flags.buffer. joints+lighting still per-frame (genuinely change). Compiles + clippy clean on ffxi-viewer-core. Zero visual/behavioral change (settings toggles still upload via the != check). Runtime perf confirmation (write_buffer/StagingBuffer count drop in /devhud or a fresh trace) still pending.

title: Zone water rendered as opaque cutout + fake blue plane, not translucent textured surface (XIM parity)
description:
Zone water is not faithful. XIM (research/xim) renders water as part of the normal textured zone mesh, with the 0x8000 material flag enabling real alpha blending (BlendFunc Src_InvSrc_Add, zBias High); ShallowWater/DeepWater TerrainType is only a collision/fishing classifier, not a render path, and there is NO separate water plane and NO UV scroll/animation.

Our client diverges in two ways:

(1) The real water surface geometry IS rendered (it lives in the MMB visual zone models, ffxi-viewer-core/src/dat_mmb.rs), but the 0x8000 translucency flag is approximated as AlphaMode::Mask(0.5) — an alpha-test CUTOUT — see submesh_alpha_mode() at ffxi-viewer-core/src/dat_mmb.rs:695-705. The reason is FfxiZoneMaterial's WGSL hardcodes output alpha = 1.0 (ffxi-viewer-core/src/zone_ffxi.wgsl:153), so it physically cannot blend. Result: water renders opaque/holey, not translucent.

(2) A separate non-faithful hack in ffxi-viewer-core/src/dat_mzb.rs:1208-1264 parses a water_height field from the MZB material chunk (ffxi-dat/src/mzb.rs:457-473) and spawns flat semi-transparent blue StandardMaterial quads sized to the placement bounding box ('N water planes spawned' in logs). XIM has no equivalent; this double-covers the real water geometry.

Faithful fix:
- Thread the 0x8000 blend flag into FfxiZoneMaterial.material_flags (flags.y is currently unused: dat_mmb.rs sets Vec4::new(has_texture,0,0,discard_threshold)).
- In zone_ffxi.wgsl fragment, emit alpha = texel.a * in.color.a when blend-mode, else 1.0 (keep opaque ground/wall fully opaque to preserve the no-holes guard documented at dat_mmb.rs:687-694).
- Set alpha_mode = AlphaMode::Blend for 0x8000 subs (XIM zBias High → likely depth_write off + transparent sort).
- Remove or debug-gate the MZB water_height blue-quad hack once translucent water geometry works.

Evidence cited inline. Area: zone-render. Vanilla parity.
notes:
CORRECTED ROOT CAUSE (my first pass was wrong): water 'not rendering at all' was NOT a material problem — the MZB water_height plane was tagged MzbNonCollisionMesh, which apply_zone_geom_visibility + compute_init_visibility keep Hidden in every zone_geom_mode except the 'All' debug mode (default mode is Off). So the water plane was invisible in normal play. There is NO water surface in the MMB visual geometry for outdoor ponds — outdoor water in our pipeline comes solely from the MZB water_height plane (XIM doesn't render outdoor water specially, which is why the Explore agent found 'no separate plane'). My initial deletion of the plane made it strictly worse (no water object at all).

Fix shipped:
- dat_mzb.rs: restored the water_height plane spawn; retagged it MzbOverlay + Visibility::Inherited and DROPPED MzbNonCollisionMesh, so it is governed only by cull_mzb_by_distance (real scene content), never the debug toggle. Material tuned toward water (translucent blue-green, roughness 0.06, reflectance 0.55) so it catches sun/sky specular instead of flat blue.
- dat_mmb.rs + zone_ffxi.wgsl (committed f607dd3): 0x8000 MMB subs -> AlphaMode::Blend with real shader alpha. This is still a valid XIM-parity improvement for genuine translucent MMB props (glass/windows) but is orthogonal to outdoor water.

Remaining (not yet done, follow-up): the plane is a bounding-box rectangle per height (crude shape, relies on terrain occlusion to clip) and is static (no ripple/scroll/reflection). A dedicated animated FfxiWaterMaterial would be the next faithfulness step.

title: Split devhud: /devhud = stage+bottom bars; per-panel toggles in in-game Debug submenu
description:
Currently a single DevHud marker + HudVerbosity::dev_hud gates ALL dev overlays (stage bar, bottom diagnostics bar, perf panel, target-cycle list, mesh-debug hover) via /devhud. Split this:
- /devhud keeps controlling stage bar (stage_bar.rs) + bottom diagnostics bar (diagnostics.rs) together (both keep the DevHud marker).
- Perf (perf_hud.rs), Target Cycle (target_list_hud.rs), Mesh Debug (mesh_debug.rs), and Net Status (network_status.rs, already /netstat) become individually toggleable from a NEW in-game 'Debug' submenu under the Commands menu (MenuKind::Debug), rows showing [on]/[off] like the Graphics submenu.
- New HudPanels resource (perf/target_cycle/mesh_debug bools) in ffxi-viewer-core::hud; menu confirm toggles it + NetStatusVisible.
hud area, enhanced? no — dev tooling. Evidence: ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/mod.rs:34-126, menu.rs, ffxi-client/src/view_native/{perf_hud.rs:248,target_list_hud.rs:27,text_input.rs:1901-2072,2256-2298}.

title: Add 'Model Shadow Casting' graphics toggle; skinned actors NotShadowCaster (perf: directional shadow spike)
description: A/B confirmed the periodic frame spike is dominated by the directional shadow pass rendering ~40 skinned NPCs across cascades (render~30-43ms, shadow cascade passes are the top per-pass at every setting; Ultra/4-cascade worse than Low/2-cascade). Retail FFXI uses blob shadows, not realtime character shadow maps. Add a NEW graphics field 'Model Shadow Casting' (separate from existing 'Model Shadows'=receive) that adds bevy::light::NotShadowCaster to skinned actor body+effect meshes when off. Default Off on Low/Medium, On on High/Ultra. Renderer: FfxiActorMeshChild marker on actor body+effect meshes (ffxi_actor_render.rs build_actor_children); apply_character_shadow_cast system inserts/removes bevy::light::NotShadowCaster (every-frame, Added query for late-spawned meshes + full sweep on settings change), registered in lib.rs ViewerCorePlugin. Settings scaffolding (field/enum/label/value_label/cycle/for_preset, committed cc8bce2) finished by adding GraphicsField::CharacterShadowCast to GRAPHICS_FIELDS and 'Model Shadow Casting' to hud::menu GRAPHICS_ENTRIES. Defaults: Off Low/Medium, On High/Ultra (quality lever tied to preset, sets Custom on toggle, resets to tier default on preset cycle). Tests: model_shadow_casting_follows_preset_tier + model_shadow_casting_is_quality_lever_tied_to_tier; graphics_entries_match_field_labels still green. 374 tests pass, clippy clean on ffxi-viewer-core. Not committed (conservative).

title: beads -> GitHub Issues publisher (create/update/close lifecycle)
description: Single one-way publisher: each bead -> one GitHub issue, keyed by an HTML-comment marker <!-- beads-id: <id> --> in the issue body. Create new, patch title/body/labels on change, close/reopen to match bead status. Replaces create-issues.sh (ROADMAP->GH). Dry-run-first. One-time migration of the 71 existing ROADMAP-derived issues (truncated titles -> close+republish) is a gated outward-facing step.
design:
Marker: <!-- beads-id: <id> --> in issue body (NOT title — titles get rewritten).
Scope: default filter = roadmap-labelled beads; --all publishes every bead.
Lifecycle per bead: no issue + open/in_progress -> create; issue exists -> patch
title/body/managed-labels + reopen/close to match status; no issue + closed -> skip.
Managed label namespace (only these are added/removed; hand labels left alone):
vanilla-parity, enhanced, area:*, status:*. Map: vanilla->vanilla-parity,
enhanced->enhanced, other bead labels->area:<x>, status open->status:missing,
in_progress->status:partial.

ONE-TIME MIGRATION (gated, outward-facing): the ~71 legacy GitHub issues from the
old ROADMAP publisher are title-keyed and truncated, so they carry no beads-id
marker. A first live publish would create fresh bead-keyed issues ALONGSIDE them
(duplicates). Plan: close the legacy set (gh issue close) then run
beads-github-publish.py for real (dry_run=false), or close legacy right after.
Then enable the push trigger in beads-github-publish.yml.

title: Periodic ~1Hz +20ms frame spike: camera_collision brute-force validation probe runs every 1s on main thread regardless of log level
description: clamp_chase_camera_to_collision (ffxi-client/src/view_native/camera_collision.rs:111-152) runs a BVH-vs-brute-force VALIDATION raycast over ALL zone+MMB collision triangles every >=1.0s. Its only output is tracing::debug!, but the expensive ray_cast_brute_force computation (lines 115-139) executed UNCONDITIONALLY, so the ~20ms cost was paid every second even at INFO log level. This is the ~36ms/1Hz spike the perf instrument flagged as 'no rebuild, model+0 plate+0' (i.e. outside the snapshot path; distinct from kuluu-p09). Fix: gate the brute-force behind tracing::enabled!(DEBUG) and time it via perf_probe::note_debug_probe so the perf HUD/log attributes it when debug is on. Found via the new perf_hud instrument (kuluu-8h9).

title: Network health HUD widget (retail S/R baud + connection % + animated arrows)
description:
Faithful reproduction of retail FFXI's top-right network indicator: two animated send/receive arrows that tint green->red, a connection-health percentage, and 'S <n>'/'R <n>' baud-rate readouts.

Research (this session):
- S=send rate, R=receive rate = BAUD RATE (data throughput), not ping/seq counts. Game engineered for ~3000 baud duplex (v.90 dial-up); crowded-zone 'R3000' = saturated receive pipe. Source: bg-wiki Draft:Network + corroborating community.
- Percentage = separate connection-health metric; 100% solid, falls on packet loss/no server response, 0% => client self-declares disconnect -> title/POL.
- LSB sends NOTHING for this: confirmed no baud/percentage field in vendor/server (preparePacket/finalizePacket, zero grep). Datagram header carries seq/ack only (MapSession.client_packet_id/server_packet_id, retransmit-on-ack-mismatch). Widget is 100% client telemetry. LSB disconnect ladder: 5s->isLinkDead, MAX_TIME_LASTUPDATE(~30-60s)->hard delete.

Decisions (agreed):
- Placement: VANILLA always-on HUD element (ffxi-viewer-core/hud/), top-right like retail, with a /netstat slash toggle to hide.
- Scope: full widget end-to-end (byte-rate plumbing + S/R + % + animated green->red arrows).
- % model: ACK-GAP based. recv_health from contiguity of inbound server seq (gaps=recv loss); send_health from server's echoed ack of our bundle_seq vs sent (lag=send loss). Overall % = min(send,recv); up-arrow color<-send_health, down-arrow color<-recv_health.

Plumbing: add on-wire byte counters at map_client.rs send/recv boundaries -> bytes/sec rolling 1s window (S/R). Add fields to state.rs Diagnostics -> wire_translate diagnostics_to_wire -> ffxi-viewer-wire Diagnostics -> read in new view widget. Reuse existing sync_in/sync_out/last_server_packet_age_ms.

Evidence: map_client.rs send_encrypted/recv_decrypted (byte boundaries known at blowfish/zlib); framing.rs Header{sync_in,sync_out}; state.rs Diagnostics; session.rs recv loop reads header.id_and_size(server seq)+sync_in(server ack of us); wire_translate.rs diagnostics_to_wire; hud/mod.rs HudPlugin+add_hud_spawners+palette+DevHud; perf_hud.rs/vana_clock.rs widget pattern.
notes:
FOLLOW-UPS since initial impl:
- b8912dc: S/R baud now refreshes on a 3s window (retail ticks ~3s, not sub-second).
- 3d50d79: widget is fixed-width (% in 36px right-justified box, S/R values in 38px boxes after static S/R prefixes) so digit-count changes don't reflow neighbors.
- 7781663: FIX — keepalive send failure no longer breaks the map loop. Previously a failed send (link down, remote server) tore the session down immediately, freezing the widget so % never decayed. Now we swallow send failures (log once on rising edge), keep ticking, and let last_recv aging drive S/R->0 (after ~3s) and %->0 (linear 3s..MAP_SILENCE_TIMEOUT=60s); silence timeout remains the single disconnect trigger. Matches retail 'decay to 0 then disconnect'. Reported by user: remote server + wifi-off showed no S/R/% change (loop had broken).

title: Reset client interaction flags on zone change (auto-run, lock-on, target, rest)
description: Client-side interaction flags leak across zone changes. AutoRun (ffxi-client/view_native/input.rs) has no reset at all; target+lock-on are only cleared incidentally by auto_clear_target_system (scene.rs:95) via entity-absence, which is fragile across per-zone unique_no collisions. Reactor goal (follow/engage/pathing) already resets to Idle on ZoneChanged. Add an explicit Update system that, on snapshot zone_id change, resets AutoRun, LockOn, Target, and RestStance — the lifecycle-symmetric resource drain at the zone boundary.
close_reason: Added reset_interaction_flags_on_zone_change (Update, InGame): on snapshot zone_id change, resets AutoRun, LockOn, Target, RestStance. Follow/engage/pathing already reset via reactor ZoneChanged->Idle. WalkMode left intact (deliberate user preference, not transient).

title: Split perf/FPS monitor out of /devhud TAB CYCLE panel into a graphical frame-time graph + spike logging
description: The /devhud TAB CYCLE panel (target_list_hud.rs) mixes tab-cycle info with perf/spike text (snap us, rebuilds/s, frame ms, spike interval/cause). Split perf out into its own panel that renders a frame-time graph (column/area history, colored by severity, with 60/30fps reference lines) and emit a throttled warn! log each time a stutter spike is detected (~16-57ms spikes every ~0.1-0.8s). Logging runs whenever InGame; graph only updates when devhud visible. Builds on kuluu-guo instrument; aids diagnosing kuluu-p09 snapshot-clone hitch.
close_reason: Implemented: perf/spike monitor split out of TAB CYCLE panel into new perf_hud.rs (frame-time column graph + throttled warn! spike logging). Compiles clean (cargo check + clippy, native-window) and fmt-passes. Runtime/visual verification pending a live LSB session.

title: Move MMB zone-prop parse onto AsyncComputeTaskPool
description: process_load_mmb_requests parses MMB props synchronously on the main thread (load_mmb: fs::read + decrypt + parse_models + decode_texture), throttled by a work budget that is really a frame-hitch guard. Mirror the MZB loader (kick/poll via AsyncComputeTaskPool): spawn load_mmb off-thread keyed by (file_id, chunk_idx), poll completed parses into MmbParseCache, and keep only the main-thread spawn work (images/meshes/materials add + entity spawn) bounded per frame. Preserve near-first priority sort, view_distance load gate, MmbParseCache/HandleCache/TexPools, diagnostics, and entity resolution. Cap concurrent in-flight parses; clear them on zone change like mzb_in_flight.
close_reason: MMB prop parse (fs read + decrypt + parse_models + DXT decode) now runs on AsyncComputeTaskPool, keyed by (file_id,chunk_idx). process_load_mmb_requests polls completed parses into MmbParseCache, then on the main thread only builds assets + spawns entities, bounded per frame (cost weighted by whether the per-file tex pool exists). Concurrent parses capped at 64; in-flight cleared on zone change alongside the queue. Near-first priority sort + view_distance gate preserved; diagnostics, caches, entity resolution unchanged. viewer-core + client build clean, clippy clean, 8 tests pass.

title: Player can move while KO'd (dead)
description: A KO'd player should be immobile (retail: you lie prone until raised/homepoint; camera still rotates). dispatch_movement_system (ffxi-client/src/view_native/input.rs:365) emits AgentCommand::Move + updates LocalPlayerPrediction from WASD with no dead guard. Add an early-out mirroring the rest_stance.is_resting() gate (line 439): when death_prompt::is_dead(&state), clear autorun and return before movement/heading is computed, leaving camera pitch/yaw/zoom intact.
notes: Fixed at the input choke point: dispatch_movement_system early-returns when death_prompt::is_dead(&state), clearing autorun, before heading/movement is computed and before LocalPlayerPrediction is advanced — so both the AgentCommand::Move to the server AND the client-side predicted movement stop, while camera pitch/yaw/zoom (handled earlier in the system) still work. NOTE: slash/agent-driven movement (/goto, MoveTo via reactor Pathing) is NOT yet gated while dead — separate, lower-priority follow-up; would need a reactor-level guard keyed on death_homepoint_secs.
close_reason: WASD/keyboard movement + local prediction blocked while KO'd via dispatch_movement_system dead-guard; camera retained. Slash/agent movement noted as follow-up.

title: Event-dialog ending is not uniform across exit paths (Esc advances instead of cancels; walk-away/range and server force-close not handled)
description:
The four ways an event-dialog can end behave inconsistently; some leave the player desynced server-side.

Paths (ffxi-client/src/view_native/text_input.rs + session.rs):
1. Option select (Enter/click): EndEventChoice -> EVENT_END sent. (EndPara value is the separate bug kuluu-jbq.)
2. Esc / NavCancel (text_input.rs:2442): view *immediately* clears snapshot.dialog + returns to World, then sends AgentCommand::EndEvent. In session.rs:1259 EndEvent calls dialog_session.advance(None). For a MESSAGE frame, None just dismisses and yields the NEXT frame (ffxi-event runner.rs:62) -> session re-emits EventDialog -> dialog_mode_sync_system (text_input.rs:283) re-opens it. So Esc on a multi-frame VM event ADVANCES instead of cancelling (dialog visibly closes then reappears). Only a menu (QUERYWAIT) frame cancels via work_zone[0]=254.
3. Esc legacy path with empty pending_event_end: session.rs:1279 else-branch only fires when pending is non-empty; if empty, NOTHING is sent. Local dialog closed but server may still consider player in-event, and the watchdog (session.rs:1708) won't fire because it keys on non-empty pending.
4. Walk away / out of range: no handler ends the event on movement. Only backstop is the 10s PENDING_EVENT_END_GRACE watchdog (session.rs:1723-1744) that force-flushes EVENT_END(option=0) and emits an Error toast (user-driven mode only; agent mode flushes every tick).
5. Server force-close: incoming 0x052 EVENTUCOFF is not decoded to clear the UI; a server-initiated event close can leave a stale local dialog.

Fix scope:
- Esc must cancel the whole event reliably (route a real cancel into the VM that ends it, not advance), and clear the UI only once the EVENT_END is actually queued.
- Decode incoming 0x052 (and/or a new event start) to clear snapshot.dialog when the server closes the event.
- Make cancel always resolve server-side (send EVENT_END for the active event id even when pending list is empty), or document why a no-op is safe.
- Consider ending the dialog cleanly on confirmed out-of-range / NPC despawn rather than relying solely on the 10s watchdog.
notes:
PARTIAL FIX LANDED (this session):
- Esc/cancel now mirrors confirm: handle_dialog_key NavCancel sends AgentCommand::EndEvent and returns None instead of optimistically clearing snapshot.dialog + forcing World mode (ffxi-client/src/view_native/text_input.rs). The session reconciles the UI via EventDialog/EventEnded -> SessionState.dialog (state.rs:821,843) and dialog_mode_sync_system. This removes the close-then-reopen flicker: a multi-frame message faithfully advances, a menu (QUERYWAIT) faithfully cancels.
- session.rs EndEvent now has an else branch emitting AgentEvent::EventEnded when there is no VM event and nothing pinned, so cancel never leaves a stuck-open local dialog (the prior empty-pending case sent nothing). No wire bytes change.

CORRECTION to original plan: do NOT consume incoming 0x052 to clear the dialog. Per vendor/server/src/map/packets/c2s/0x05b_eventend.cpp:71, the server pushes GP_SERV_COMMAND_EVENTUCOFF after EVERY 0x5B process including Mode=UpdatePending (mid-menu) — so it is an ack, not a close signal; consuming it would wrongly close multi-step menus.

STILL OPEN:
- Walk-away / out-of-range still relies only on the 10s PENDING_EVENT_END_GRACE watchdog; no proactive end on confirmed movement/NPC despawn.
- A genuine server-initiated event close (distinct from the EVENTUCOFF ack) is not separately signalled/handled.

title: Distance-prioritized + view-distance-gated MMB zone-prop streaming
description:
The MMB zone-placement load queue (dat_mmb.rs process_load_mmb_requests) drains FIFO at 48 work-units/frame regardless of where the player is, and nothing visibility-culls loaded MmbOverlay props (cull_mzb_by_distance is defined but unregistered; only cull_entities_by_distance runs). Result: zone-in loads props in arbitrary order and far props render until zone change.

Add: (1) sort MmbLoadQueue.pending nearest-player-first each frame (entity/look-driven spawns keep priority, zone-spawns sorted by XZ-dist2); (2) defer zone-spawn loads beyond draw.world*margin (re-checked as player moves, never dropped); (3) cull_mmb_props_by_distance visibility system at draw.world so far loaded props stop rendering, with load margin>cull radius for hysteresis. Despawn-unload intentionally skipped: mesh/texture/material handles persist in MmbParseCache/MmbHandleCache/MmbTexPools for the whole zone, so despawn frees little while adding reload thrash.
notes: v2 after playtest: load gate now driven by GraphicsSettings.view_distance (was DrawDistance.world=80, far too low and not the setting users control). Removed cull_mmb_props_by_distance entirely — the camera far plane (= view_distance, settings.rs:1068) already frustum-culls props beyond the visible range, so the extra distance-cull was redundant and was the source of the pop-in/pop-out at 80 units. Net: load nearest-first within view_distance*1.25 (lead ring), render bounded by far plane, no visibility toggling.
close_reason: Shipped v2: MMB zone-prop queue sorts nearest-player-first and defers loads beyond GraphicsSettings.view_distance*1.25 (re-checked as player moves). Rendering bounds left to the camera far plane; redundant distance-cull removed. fmt/clippy/tests green.

title: Death/resurrect music+combat lifecycle (dead-slot, self-death disengage)
description: Reported: dying did not play death music; after resurrect the character stayed engaged (stance + now battle music) until /disengage. Root causes found and two fixed: (1) audio dead detection used icons.contains(EFFECT_KO=0) which is wrong — KO is not a status-effect icon; fixed to self_entity.is_dead() (hp_pct==0, wire Entity helper). (2) reactor Goal::Engaged only disengaged when the TARGET died (reactor.rs:597), never on self death; added self_is_dead() check so death forces Goal::Idle (+test self_death_disengages_even_if_target_alive). REMAINING/OPEN: (a) Dead music slot 5 is never populated — zone-in 0x0A only carries slots 0-4 and LSB only sets slot 5 via scripted Lua changeMusic, so a normal field death has no track; faithful behavior is battle-music-stops→zone-music. Decision needed: vanilla (server-driven only) vs enhanced hardcoded death jingle. (b) Bevy ECS WARN 'Entity despawned generation N' double-despawn around homepoint warp (InGameEntity zone-cleanup vs AudioFade despawn) — separate lifecycle hardening.
notes: Self-death state now reliable. ROOT: self KO is signaled only via 0x037 CHAR_STATUS (hpp==0) -> death_homepoint_secs; the self entity hp_pct and (when solo) the party row never reach 0, so the renderer's self_dead (party-row) + audio dead (entity) never fired. FIX: DeathTimerUpdated now carries Option<u32> (Some=KO w/ homepoint secs, None=alive) so CHAR_STATUS both sets AND clears death (fixes raise-in-place stuck-dead; zone-change already cleared). Renderer self_dead (ffxi_actor_render.rs:1654) and audio dead now read death_homepoint_secs.is_some() (||existing party-row/entity). Reactor disengage gated to Some(_) so an alive CHAR_STATUS doesn't disengage (test revive_event_does_not_disengage). Diagnostic log now prints dead + death_homepoint_secs. NOTE: vanilla has no field death MUSIC (slot 5 empty) — battle->zone on death is correct; this fix is about the death ANIMATION + correct dead flag.

title: Battle music doesn't trigger on engage (zone 116, slot 2/3)
description: Engaging solo in zone 116 (East Sarutabaruta) does not switch to battle music, despite zone_settings defining battlesolo=101/battlemulti=103. The full slot infrastructure (BgmSlots, resolve_audible_slot, 0x0A MusicNum[5], 0x5F) is already built and retail-faithful. The suspect is derive_bgm_playback_state (ffxi-viewer-core/src/audio.rs:221): engagement is gated solely on self_entity.bt_target_id != 0, which depends on the LSB server echoing the self CHAR_PC update with a battle target. The self status byte is hardcoded to 0 (session.rs:688), so it can't be a fallback. The reactor already tracks engagement authoritatively as current_goal == Engaged (carried in the snapshot, wire/lib.rs:325) but the audio system ignores it. Step 1: add change-detected debug logging to derive_bgm_playback_state exposing bt_target_id, current_goal, and self status, then engage in zone 116 to confirm which signal is live. Step 2: drive battle music from the reliable signal (likely current_goal primary, bt_target_id fallback).
notes: FIXED engage trigger: derive_bgm_playback_state now drives engaged from reactor current_goal==Engaged (||bt_target), since LSB never echoes the self bt_target (confirmed via live log: engaged_signal=false self_bt_target_id=0 reactor_goal_engaged=true). Extracted self_engaged() helper + regression test reactor_goal_drives_engaged_without_server_bt_target. Awaiting live re-test in a zone with distinct battle music (116 = East Sarutabaruta, battlesolo=101).
close_reason: Engage->battle music fixed (current_goal drives engaged) and verified live: zone 240 transition showed engaged_solo:true selecting CombatSolo slot. Live confirmation track was 151 because Windurst's combat slots == zone track; engage detection itself confirmed working.

title: In-app self-update download+replace+restart behind a self-update feature
description: Behind feature `self-update`, implement apply_update() via the self_update crate's github backend consuming dist's asset scheme ffxi-client-<tag>-<triple>.{tar.gz,zip}. Download-to-temp, verify SHA256, atomic replace, prompt restart. macOS: do NOT strip/re-sign client-side — the released artifact's signature must survive or the binary is killed:9. Disable for package-manager installs. Depends on signed artifacts existing so updates are trustworthy.

title: Windows Authenticode signing via Azure Trusted Signing owned by dist
description: User chose Azure Trusted Signing (~$10/mo, EV-class, no HSM, signs in CI). Drive it from dist as the single Windows-signing owner (NOT a parallel hand-rolled leg). Mandatory RFC3161 timestamp http://timestamp.acs.microsoft.com so signatures survive cert rotation. Requires AZURE_TENANT_ID/CLIENT_ID/CLIENT_SECRET/SUBSCRIPTION_ID + TRUSTED_SIGNING_ENDPOINT/ACCOUNT_NAME/CERT_PROFILE_NAME and completed Azure Trusted Signing identity validation (several business days — start early). BLOCKED on user.

title: macOS Developer ID sign + notarize + staple as a dist post-strip custom job
description: dist has NO macOS notarization. Add a dist local-artifacts/post-build job running AFTER dist's strip: import Developer ID Application cert into a throwaway keychain, codesign --options runtime --timestamp, ditto into a .zip, xcrun notarytool submit --wait with an App Store Connect API key, xcrun stapler staple. title: [bug] Character/entity Y-bob flicker: floor-snap one-sided cutoff oscillates
description: snap_entities_to_mzb_floor_system used ground_raycast (highest floor <= Y + STEP_TOLERANCE 2.0). The server reference Y and MZB floor disagree (~5-10y), so true ground sat at the cutoff and sub-unit per-frame Y wobble flipped it in/out -> body bobbed each frame. Fixed with MzbCollisionGeometry::ground_nearest (closest floor to Y, a fixed point under grounding).
close_reason: Fixed this session: 35efe13 (mipmap actor textures), 7635184 (ground_nearest), 1576079 (windowed lamp falloff + softened base).

title: [bug] Character models shimmer whole-body under camera motion (actor textures lack mipmaps)
description: ffxi_actor_render::decoded_texture_to_image built character textures with bare Image::new (mip_level_count=1, default sampler) while zone/MMB textures go through zone_texture's sRGB mip chain + anisotropic sampler. No mips => texture minification aliasing shimmers the whole textured body under any camera motion; crisp when camera truly static. Fixed by routing actor textures through new zone_texture::image_with_mips (mips on, aniso 8), alpha left as decoder produced it.
close_reason: Fixed this session: 35efe13 (mipmap actor textures), 7635184 (ground_nearest), 1576079 (windowed lamp falloff + softened base).

title: [bug] Minimap blank after zone-in: image-source change-detection edge consumed before MinimapImage node exists
description:
Symptom: minimap renders dots but no map texture right after zone-in / game load; re-issuing '/minimap cull 6' (forces a top-down re-bake) reliably makes the map appear.

Root cause: update_minimap_image_source (ffxi-viewer-core/src/minimap/mod.rs:406) is gated on '!state.is_changed() && !mode.is_changed()'. The retail/top-down image becomes available via a single MinimapState change, but on that frame the system early-returns at a LATER guard (q.single_mut() Err because the MinimapImage node from OnEnter(InGame) isn't present yet, or retail_image is momentarily None during the zone-change clear). That consumes the change edge; the gate then short-circuits forever and the ImageNode.image handle is never assigned until the next bake mutates MinimapState. The overlay (dots) and crop systems run unconditionally so they stay correct — only the image handle is stuck on the transparent placeholder, showing the dark BACKGROUND.

Fix: drop the is_changed() early-return in update_minimap_image_source so it idempotently re-evaluates and assigns the resolved handle every frame (the existing 'image_node.image != h' check keeps it cheap and churn-free). update_minimap_crop_rect already runs unconditionally on the same query, so this is consistent.
close_reason: Dropped the is_changed() early-return in update_minimap_image_source (ffxi-viewer-core/src/minimap/mod.rs) so the resolved minimap handle is re-evaluated/assigned every frame (idempotent via image_node.image != h). Fixes blank map after zone-in where the single MinimapState change edge was consumed before the MinimapImage node existed. Build + 20 minimap tests pass, clippy/fmt clean.

title: [bug] Dead entities (hp_pct==0) remain tab-cyclable and click-targetable
description: Dead targets should not be cyclable via Tab nor selectable via click. build_tab_candidates (ffxi-client/src/view_native/input.rs) and click_to_target_system (ffxi-viewer-core/src/picking.rs) do not filter dead entities; auto_clear_target_system already drops a target on death (hp_pct==Some(0)) but selection still lands on corpses first. Fix: centralize an Entity::is_targetable() predicate in ffxi-viewer-wire and apply it to tab-cycle candidates, click picking, and should_clear_target. Open question: which other states (cutscene/event NPCs, despawning) should also be excluded.
notes: Implemented. Centralized Entity::{is_dead,is_targetable,is_cycle_candidate} in ffxi-viewer-wire. Decisions (user): (1) dead PCs stay click-targetable for Raise, but no corpse cycles via Tab; dead mobs/NPCs fully blocked. (2) Also exclude LSB STATUS_TYPE hide states {2,3,4,6,18,20} (verified vs vendor/server/.../baseentity.h; CUTSCENE_ONLY=6 not 4). Fixed a latent bug found by protocol-conformance-reviewer: Entity.status was only decoded under UPDATE_HP(0x04), but LSB writes status every tick (UPDATE_STATUS=0x02 changes never set 0x04), so status went stale. Split status into a top-level session Entity field decoded every tick (NpcState::decode_char_npc_status); npc_state still carries UPDATE_HP-gated animation/animationsub. Call sites: build_tab_candidates uses is_cycle_candidate; click_to_target_system + should_clear_target use is_targetable. Tests added in ffxi-viewer-wire, input.rs, scene.rs, decode.rs, state.rs.
close_reason: Implemented + unit-tested: dead/hidden entities non-cyclable; dead PCs stay click-targetable for Raise; STATUS_TYPE {2,3,4,6,18,20} excluded; fixed status-staleness (decode every tick). Live in-game verification pending.

title: EPIC: retail particle-based clouds (faithful weather sky effects)
description:
Make Vanilla (and Enhanced) clouds use retail's actual cloud rendering instead of the procedural FBM layer (which is Enhanced-only today; Vanilla currently has NO clouds). Per research/xim + real-DAT probing this session:

GROUNDED FACTS (validated against zone DAT file 101 via examples/dat-cloud-probe.rs):
- Retail clouds are 0x05 ParticleGenerators 'cld1'/'cld2' living in weat/<weathertype>/ (fine, clod, suny, mist, thdr, ...). attach=0x5, linked visual = StaticMesh (0x1F ParticleMesh), linked id 'cld_' (fine) / 'clod' (cloudy). Two stacked layers: cld1 base=[0,0,0], cld2 base=[0,30,0]. The sun is the sibling gen 'sun1' (attach=0xE Sun, mesh 'suns'); retail lens flare is gen 'lf03' (attach=0xE, linkedType LensFlare).
- 0x1F == our ChunkKind::D3m; d3m.rs ALREADY parses it (D3m{ num_triangles, texture_name[16], vertices[pos,color,uv] }). 16-byte texture name splits into category+id like the moon.
- CRITICAL UNKNOWN: the cld_/clod/suns 0x1F meshes + their textures are NOT in the per-zone DAT (only the generators are) nor in the old moon-candidate files (55540/55660/55780/56200). They live in a shared GLOBAL effects DAT (mirrors XIM's global texture/mesh fallback). Must locate it.
- Sky gradient is already retail-faithful (skybox.wgsl samples 8-color/8-altitude weather-DAT gradient). The gap is purely clouds.

REUSE: generator.rs (0x05 header: section offsets @0x74/0x78, StandardParticleSetup opcode 0x01 carries linkedDataId@+8, base_position@+16, linkedDataType@+29), d3m.rs (0x1F mesh), map_image::scan_graphics + texture decode (proven on the moon), walk_tree (dir-aware), weather_fx.rs (Weather enum + particle infra). 0x05 opcode block: byte0=opcode, byte1&0x1F=size_words, len=size_words*4, payload@+4.

See child tasks for milestones. Dev tools added this session: examples/dat-cloud-probe.rs (dumps 0x05 gens by dir + 0x1F meshes), examples/dat-spritesheet-probe.rs.

title: Tab-target list empty at normal camera pitch in hilly zones (W. Ronfaure): vertical transform sign flip
description: Repro: in W. Ronfaure (trees + height differentials) the Tab target list is empty until the camera is pitched top-down. Root cause: build_tab_candidates (ffxi-client/src/view_native/input.rs:821) computed the projection point as Vec3::new(e.pos.x, e.pos.z, -e.pos.y) — the vertical (Bevy-up) component had the WRONG SIGN vs the canonical ffxi_to_bevy (ffxi-viewer-core/src/scene.rs:14 = Vec3::new(p.x, -p.z, -p.y)). Every entity was projected at a mirrored height; at normal pitch the bad ndc.y fell outside CYCLE_NDC_Y_LIMIT(1.6) so all entities were culled, but top-down the vertical barely affects screen xy so they reappeared. Existing cycle tests all used z:0.0 so the sign was never exercised. Fix: use ffxi_viewer_core::ffxi_to_bevy(e.pos); added regression test candidate_projects_at_canonical_grounded_height pinning the vertical transform. Follow-up for fuller retail parity: cycle uses screen-space NDC culling, which still drops valid nearby targets at extreme camera pitch and requires on-screen framing; retail selects by world-space range + forward arc (heading-relative), pitch-independent and no LOS/occlusion requirement.
close_reason: Tab-target vertical sign-flip fix + regression test verified already present in main (input.rs:822); bead was stale-open

title: Enhanced sky over-reddens at dusk (atmosphere multiplied by saturated sun-light color)
description:
In Enhanced sky mode the whole sky dome turns deep maroon near sunset (~V17:00), not just the horizon. Root cause: graphics_settings::apply_physical_sky_system inserts Bevy Atmosphere::earthlike + AtmosphereSettings::default on the camera (ffxi-viewer-core/src/graphics_settings.rs:1196-1204). Bevy's atmosphere uses the sun DirectionalLight's color*illuminance as incident sun radiance, so it multiplies in-scattered sky light by the light color. sun_moon::sun_moon_system sets that light to sun_color_for_hour() (sun_moon.rs:392-398), which at dusk is heavily saturated (~rgb(1.0,0.41,0.30) at hour 17 — sun_color_for_hour, sun_moon.rs:207-229). That red tint is applied on TOP of the atmosphere's own wavelength-dependent reddening => maroon everywhere.

Fix direction: in physical/Enhanced mode feed the atmosphere a near-white (physical) sun light color and let the scattering produce the sunset gradient; leave the Vanilla gradient path (driven by weather-DAT keyframes) untouched since it looks correct. Likely needs a small live tuning pass on the whiten amount and possibly AtmosphereSettings (scene_units_to_m).
notes: Implemented in 47cce4b. sun_moon_system now whitens the sun DirectionalLight color toward white (lerp 0.85) when settings.sky_style.physical() (Enhanced), so Bevy's atmosphere isn't fed a saturated dusk tint. Vanilla gradient path untouched. PENDING: visual verification — confirm dusk sky is no longer uniformly maroon; the 0.85 whiten factor is a one-number tune in sun_moon.rs if it needs more/less warmth.

title: Lens-flare sun glare drifts off the sun disc (overscan vs screen-UV mismatch)
description:
In Vanilla sky mode the sun's god-rays (halo/streak/ghosts) render vertically offset from the sun disc, most visibly near sunrise/sunset when the sun sits away from screen centre.

Root cause: the lens-flare quad is intentionally oversized by FLARE_OVERSCAN=1.15 (ffxi-viewer-core/src/lens_flare.rs:53,132) so it always covers the frustum. But the shader used the quad's in.uv as the screen coordinate (ffxi-viewer-core/src/lens_flare.wgsl:109) while comparing against 'sun', a TRUE screen UV from the projection (lens_flare.wgsl:75-77). A quad 15% larger than the frustum maps in.uv to screen [-0.075,1.075], so every flare element drew at 0.5+(sun-0.5)*1.15 -> offset 0.15*(sun-0.5). Sun above centre (sun.y<0.5) pushes the glare up by ~0.03 of screen height (~33px), vanishing only when the sun is dead-centre.

Not a sun_dir/look_at issue: disc and flare share the identical sun_dir and radius 4000, and OperatorCamera is unparented so view.world_position==cam_pos; the world point is the same.

Fix: derive screen UV from the fragment framebuffer coord instead of the overscanned quad UV: uv = (in.position.xy - view.viewport.xy)/max(view.viewport.zw,1). Keeps the overscan coverage margin while landing the flare exactly on the projected sun. Reuses the same view.viewport convention the occlusion taps already use (lens_flare.wgsl:85).
close_reason: Fixed in lens_flare.wgsl: screen UV now derived from framebuffer coord, so the overscanned flare quad no longer drifts the glare off the sun disc.

title: /logout should return to character select, not the launcher front (retail parity); differentiate /logout vs /shutdown destinations
description:
Retail: /logout returns to the CHARACTER SELECT screen (account session retained, you can pick another character); /shutdown returns to the title/POL/server-select screen. Our client collapses both into 'back to the front of the launcher'.

Evidence:
- AppPhase has only {Launcher, Connecting, InGame} (mod.rs:60-68). There is no character-select phase; char select is LauncherState::CharList, a SubState of Launcher (launcher_ui/mod.rs:67-104, 667-690).
- return_to_launcher_on_disconnect sets AppPhase::Launcher (mod.rs:472-473), which re-enters at the default substate Login and runs decide_initial_screen (launcher_ui/mod.rs:877-927) -> server/account select. CharList is never the destination.
- The full connect path consumes the lobby handoff: CharList -> ConnectInFlight -> Done -> advance_to_connecting -> AppPhase::Connecting -> bridge_connecting (mod.rs:506-594). OpenedLobby (launcher_ui/mod.rs:427-434) holds the lobby handle + auth session but is not kept alive / re-established after entering the map.

Faithful behavior requires returning to CharList without re-authenticating, i.e. retaining the AuthSession and re-opening the lobby/view connection on /logout (the map server hands the client back to the lobby). /shutdown keeps current behavior (full drop to login/server select).

Depends on disentangling the /exit-vs-disconnect race tracked separately (the disconnect-watcher currently can't tell quit-the-app from logout-to-char-select). Likely needs: distinguish DisconnectKind (UserLogout vs Shutdown vs Forced) from the wire/agent reason, and a return-to-CharList path that reuses the retained lobby session.
acceptance_criteria: /logout (LeaveGame) returns to the character-select list with the account session intact; /shutdown returns to login/server select; a Forced disconnect still returns to launcher with the error toast.

title: Periodic render hitch: full SceneSnapshot deep-clone on render thread per snapshot change
description: Symptom: ~1-2s periodic frame hitch even at max FPS. Root cause (code-evidenced, not yet profiler-confirmed): ffxi-client/src/session.rs:2028 run_event_folder calls state_tx.send_modify() on EVERY AgentEvent; the render thread's poll_snapshot (ffxi-client/src/view_native/bridge.rs:25) responds to any watch change by deep-cloning the ENTIRE SessionState into a SceneSnapshot via state_to_snapshot (ffxi-client/src/wire_translate.rs:9): entities/party/chat/status_icons/recasts/spell+ability lists/inventory/equipment, then Box-allocates it. Amplified by the state.dirty O(n) fan-out (ffxi-viewer-core/src/snapshot.rs ingest + scene.rs sync_* systems). Server traffic (keepalive + nearby-entity position/HP updates) clusters ~1Hz, riding the beat. Tab-targeting ruled OUT (picking.rs is event-driven; no timed target rebuild). Fix direction: drive the render side off incremental SceneDelta (drain_deltas already exists at bridge.rs:34 but returns empty) instead of a full clone-and-rebuild per change; or hash/coalesce to skip no-op rebuilds. Confirmation instrument added to /devhud (snap us / rebuilds-per-sec / frame-max ms in target-list panel).
notes: Live /devhud data refined the diagnosis: snap rebuild is only ~6us at n=7 (cheap) and rebuilds/s exceeds 60 -> the watch channel reports changed EVERY frame (send_modify per AgentEvent on the Tokio side), so the render thread does a full clone + state.dirty O(n) fan-out every frame = continuous baseline waste, but NOT the periodic 1-2s spike (frame max ~24ms observed on non-rebuild-cost frames). Added a frame-spike detector + correlation counters (ffxi-viewer-core/src/perf_probe.rs: model_loads, nameplate_rasters) bumped at load_pc/load_npc and the nameplate rasterize site; /devhud panel now shows spike magnitude, ~interval, and on-spike model/nameplate/rebuild correlation to pinpoint the periodic culprit. Two problems: (1) per-frame full rebuild [delta path], (2) the still-unidentified ~1-2s spike [pending live correlation read].

Shows ordered candidates with index, name#id, kind, distance, party/pet tier tag, and highlights the current target. Helps diagnose targeting + verify whether the cycle rebuild is implicated in periodic renderer stutter.

Compiles, clippy-clean, tab-cycle unit tests pass. Not yet run live (needs server+DAT).

title: Player ↔ player trade window
description:
Player ↔ player trade window — 4×2 grid + gil selector + quantity + OK/Cancel

Grounded 2026-06-14: verified [~] (claimed [x]). title: Weaponskill chain UI
description:
Weaponskill chain UI — weaponskill dispatch exists; the vanilla skillchain message + animation display is not confirmed

Grounded 2026-06-14: verified [~] (claimed [~]). Weaponskill dispatch is fully wired (ActionKind::Weaponskill -> action ID 0x07 across slash command, menu, MCP, relay). The action-packet (battle2) decode does read the has_proc sub-result and route its proc_message through build_battle2_line into a Battle chat line, so a skillchain proc message *number* would render generically; but the skillchain element kind (ActionProcSkillChain Light/Distortion/etc.) is explicitly discarded (`let _proc_kind = br.read(6);`), and the weaponskill *animation* is not wired — action_routine only maps categories 1/7/8/9/10/12 and never category 3 (SkillFinish), so no weaponskill-finish animation plays. Real scaffolding with an incomplete display side matches the claimed [~].
Evidence:
- ffxi-client/src/session.rs:2210 — proc (added-effect/skillchain) kind is decoded then discarded: `let _proc_kind = br.read(6);` — only proc_message number is used, so the skillchain element name (Light/Distortion/...) is never resolved into the displayed message.
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/ffxi_actor_render.rs:994 — action_routine maps action_kind 1/7/8/9/10/12 only (BasicAttack/SkillStart/MagicStart/ItemStart/AbilityStart/RangedStart); category 3 SkillFinish (the weaponskill itself) and all *finish* categories fall through to `_ => return None`, so no weaponskill animation overlay is triggered.
- ffxi-client/src/state.rs:1332 — Weaponskill dispatch is fully wired: ActionKind::Weaponskill maps to action ID 0x07 and fills skill_id; reachable via /ws slash command (slash_commands.rs:368), HUD menu (hud/menu.rs:339), MCP tool, and relay.
- vendor/server/src/map/enums/action/proc_kind.h:71 — Authoritative LSB ActionProcSkillChain enum (Light=1..Umbra=16) is the value carried in the 6-bit proc_kind the client discards — confirms the skillchain element display gap is real, not runtime-only.

Also 0x1e is Set Color per POLUtils (a formatting/color code), not visible text, so it should be stripped like {Auto} rather than shown as {Element:N}.
