title: Thread event-message parameter array through the VM for {Num:N}/{Item:N}/{KeyItem:N}/{ChocoboName:N} substitution
description: EventMessage only carries message_id; the numeric/item/choice parameters the dialog control codes reference (0x0A Num, 0x0C Choice, 0x19 Item, 0x1A KeyItem) are never extracted, so {Num:N}/{Item:N}/{KeyItem:N} markers leak and {Choice:N} defaults to alternative 0 (see kuluu-e2v). Extract the message parameter list from the message opcode in ffxi-event vm.rs, carry it on EventMessage/EventChoice, and resolve all param-driven markers in the render layer with real values.
notes: Naming discrepancy FIXED in kuluu-9xi (0x1a=KeyItem, 0x1c=ChocoboName, 0x1e=SetColor now match POLUtils; SetColor stripped as formatting). REMAINING: EventMessage/EventChoice only carry message_id; extract the message parameter list from the message opcode in ffxi-event vm.rs so the param-driven markers ({Num:N}, {Item:N}, {KeyItem:N}, {ChocoboName:N}) resolve to real values, and so {Choice:N} uses the real selector instead of defaulting to alternative 0 (UNRESOLVED_CHOICE_ALT in ffxi-event/src/runner.rs).

title: Fountains & waterfalls: zone-static particle generators (gravity spray + UV-scroll sheet)
description:
We render no fountains/waterfalls yet. Per research (research/xim) these are zone-static Generator-chunk effects, registered once at zone load (XIM Scene.kt:126 registerEffect(ZoneAssociation)), anchored at the generator base_position — same DAT family that drives braziers/lamp point-lights (ffxi-dat/src/generator.rs). Three render parts:

1. Fountain jet + waterfall splash = particle generator: emits billboard particles (frames_per_emission/particles_per_emission) with initial upward/radial velocity, then a section-3 VelocityAccelerator (gravity) opcode arcs the spray back down. The gravity/velocity-accel sec3 updater is NOT applied yet — tracked in kuluu-8i3. XIM evidence: ParticleDrawer.kt:227 ('needed for Ro'Maeve's fountain effects'); research/xim/src/Notes.txt (Metalworks + N.San d'Oria fountains).

2. Waterfall sheet = textured mesh with scrolling UVs (TextureCoordinateUpdater sec3 0x27/0x28 -> V scroll). Parsing landed: ffxi-dat/src/generator.rs:99-250 (uv_scroll); application at render time (particle_sim.rs) is the remaining gap (kuluu-8i3).

3. Same scrolling-UV mechanism overlaps the river/flat-water surface work (kuluu-980).

Concrete gaps: sec3 VelocityAccelerator (gravity) not applied; UV-scroll parsed but not applied in render; zone-static fountain/waterfall generators not placed/spawned. Blocked on the particle VM sec3/sec4 work (kuluu-8i3). Area: zone-render. Vanilla parity.

title: Render scale: FSR1 (EASU+RCAS) WGSL upscale pass to replace bilinear
description: Follow-up to kuluu-aoc. The render-scale composite currently upscales the off-screen 3D image with a plain bilinear sampler (ImageNode + ImageSampler::linear in ffxi-viewer-core/src/graphics/render_scale.rs). Replace with an FSR1 spatial upscaler (EASU edge-adaptive upsample + RCAS sharpen) as a custom WGSL material/post-process on the composite camera, for far better quality at <100% scale. FSR1 is MIT/public-domain shader code with no temporal/motion-vector dependency, so it runs cross-platform (Metal/Vulkan/DX/web) — ideal for the macOS + Steam Deck (AMD) targets where Bevy's DLSS can't run. Register the material like FfxiZoneMaterial (embedded:// wgsl, MaterialPlugin).

Bilinear first, FSR1 (EASU+RCAS) WGSL pass as a follow-up. Cross-platform (Metal/Vulkan/DX/web); the only AI upscaler in Bevy 0.18 (DLSS) is RTX+Vulkan-only so it does not help the macOS dev box or the AMD Steam Deck target.

Evidence / wiring points:
- Graphics GUI is the launcher pre-login screen: ffxi-client/src/view_native/launcher_ui/graphics.rs (spawn_field_row), driven by GraphicsSettings Resource + GraphicsField enum + GRAPHICS_FIELDS in ffxi-viewer-core/src/graphics_settings.rs (TAA = AaMode::Taa is the template option, applied by apply_anti_aliasing_system ~L934 which respawns build_operator_camera).
- Persistence: ffxi-client/src/graphics_store.rs (JSON at ~/.config/ffxi-mcp/graphics.json; auto via Serialize on GraphicsSettings).
- Camera: ffxi-viewer-core/src/camera.rs build_operator_camera (RenderLayers 0 + gizmo). HUD is Bevy UI on layer 0 of this same camera (ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/mod.rs) => need a separate native-res composite/UI camera; 3D camera renders to the low-res image.

- Slash command: add /renderscale to COMMANDS + SlashOutcome in ffxi-client/src/view_native/slash_commands.rs (mirror parse_minimap/parse_devhud) and dispatch in view_native/mod.rs.
- Upscale shader: new embedded WGSL material alongside ffxi-viewer-core/src/zone_ffxi.wgsl etc., registered in lib.rs.

Gotchas: picking (ffxi-viewer-core/src/picking.rs) and nameplate billboards (ffxi-viewer-core/src/nameplate_billboard.rs) project via the 3D camera; with a low-res target their viewport coords are in low-res space and must be scaled by render_scale to stay aligned with the native-res HUD. Minimap is already render-layer-isolated (no change). Also establish/enforce the convention that NEW graphics options must be surfaced in the launcher graphics GUI.

title: Vanilla moon should use retail 12-frame sprite sheet, not procedural shader
description:
Vanilla sky draws the moon with a procedural shader (ffxi-viewer-core/src/moon.wgsl: hash noise + 3 gaussian maria + an in-shader ellipse terminator for phase; driven by MoonMaterial params in moon_material.rs). Retail FFXI instead draws the moon as a 12-frame sprite sheet, one baked image per phase, indexed by phase (confirmed via research/xim: EnvironmentManager.MoonPhase 0..11; MoonPhaseSpriteSheetUpdater sets particle.spriteSheetIndex; SpriteSheetMeshProvider returns spriteSheet.meshes[index]). Sprite sheet lives in environment DAT Section 0x21 (SpriteSheetMesh) referenced from Section 0x2F (EnvironmentSection), textures in Section 0x20.

Current infra (per audit): ffxi-dat texture.rs decodes DXT1/DXT3/palettized/0xA1/raw; zone_texture::decoded_texture_to_image bridges to bevy Image. ffxi-dat/src/weather.rs parses ONLY the 176-byte lighting/color header of 0x2F (skybox_colors/altitudes/fog/light), NOT the sprite-sheet refs. Section 0x21 is NOT in kind.rs and not parsed. moon_material.rs currently loads a single static texture by hardcoded file ids [55660,55780,56200,55540] via map_image::scan_graphics — a stopgap, not the phase sprite sheet.

Work: add ChunkKind 0x21 + sprite_sheet.rs parser (frame count, per-frame texture/UV), walk 0x2F->0x21->0x20 to locate the moon sprite sheet, expose 12 frames to the renderer, drive frame by VanaSky.moon phase, and have Vanilla moon sample the chosen frame instead of the procedural path. ~500-800 LOC + reverse-engineering the 0x21 binary layout. Re-express XIM logic in our own code; do not copy.
notes: Implemented in 47cce4b. ffxi-dat: ChunkKind::SpriteSheet(0x21) + sprite_sheet.rs::extract_moon_sprite_sheet (12-frame moonshap sheet -> UV rects + matched 256x256 texture via scan_graphics). Format validated against real DATs (dat-spritesheet-probe example) and research/xim. ffxi-viewer-core: MoonSpriteFrames resource + zone-driven loader in moon_material.rs (FFXI half-scale alpha expanded), moon.wgsl samples the phase frame's sub-rect, sun_moon_system selects frame via moon_phase_frame() (unit-tested 0=new/6=full). Procedural disc remains indoor fallback. PENDING: visual verification on running client — confirm (a) correct phase frame shows, (b) crescent waxing/waning direction (u-axis) not mirrored, (c) opacity looks right. Quick tweaks if off: flip u or v in moon.wgsl sprite branch; adjust alpha_remap.
